California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornmission
A. Kay vinson
city of Barstow
220 East Mountain view street
Barstow, CA 92 311-998I
Dear Ms
vinson:
we have received your
of interest provisions of
September L7, ].99 L
Re:
Your Request for Advice
our FiLe No. A-91-355
request for advice. under ihe conflici
the Political 
Reform Act (ahe ".\ct").1/
OUESTIONS
1. Does the City of Barstcw's revised ccnilict of interes"
code conform to the requirements of the Political Reforn Act?
2- Under the revised code, must the chief of Police disclose
as a business position his advisory board posicion with a local
bank?
CONCLUS ION S
1. The conflict of interest code does nots apPear to be
compJ.ete in that it fails to set out the standard provisions
required by Government Code section 873O2 or, i.n lhe alternative.
fails to incorporate Commission Regulation 2 caI- Code of Regs.
section 14710.
2. The tertns of the conflict of interest code do not require
the chief of Polj.ce tc dj.scLose his advisory board position with a
locaI bank.
1/ co.r".n."nt code Sections 8l.ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Government Code unLess otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regu.Lations appear at 2 california Code of Regutations section
18000, et seo. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Di.vision 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
-128 J streer, Suite 8oo . P.o. Box so7 o Sacramenro cA 958o4-ogoz . (916) j zz-j66o


September 17′
 199■
Page Two
FACTS
In May of 1991, the chief of Police for the city of Barstow
wrote to the commission asking if he was required to disclose his
position on an advisory board to a local bank as a business
position on his statement of economic interests. 
He was advised
that since the city's conflict of interest code at that time did
not require the disclosure of business positions, such disclosure
was unnecessary. (Sessions Advice Letter, No. I-91-221.)
In your July 18, 1991 letter you indicated that the city of
Barstow took action on JuLy 15, 1991 to revise the conflict of
interest code. It now requires the chief of Pol'ice to disclose
investments and business positions in business entities and income
from sources which provide services, suppl'ies, materials,
machinery or equipment of the type utilized by the chi.ef 's
department.
In your letter you further indica"ed that the particular
bank, of which the police chief is an advisory menber, dces no"
contract or provide services to the police chief's CeparEnent'.
The ciEy of Barstow does ho;ever u'.iIi:e ihe ser'/ices cf the banl:,
but the police chief's depar-.nent independencl'/ dces nct''
OISCUSS ION
The PoI j.tical Reform Act requires every agency to adopt a
conflict of interest code. (Section 87300-) A conflict of
interest code must specificalLy enumerate each of the positions
within the agency which make or particiPate in the making of
decisions which may foreseeab).y have a material effect on any
financial interest. 
Further the code must set forth the type of
investnents, business positions, income and real proPerty !,hich
must be rePorted by those designated employees. The tyPes of
financial lnteresti which must be reported are the types which the
designated employee can affect by hii or her decision making.
( Section 87302. )
In addition to enuneratj.ng positions desj.gnated in the code
and providi.ng corresponding disclosure categories, conflict of
interest codes must specifically set out alL of the provisions
described in Section 87302(b) . For example, Section 87302(b)
requires codes to specify such things as when the statements are
to be fi1ed, what information is to be disclosed on those
statements. and t hat the reporting period would be for each
statenent. Sectj,on 87302(b) provides that the code must also set
out the disqualification provisions as defined by Section 87103.
Rather than try to extract all of this infornation from the Act,
the Conmission encourages governnental agencies to incorporate the
provisions of its Regulation 187t0.


september 17′  1991
Page Three
while the conflict of interest code for the city of Barstow
designates the positions whi.ch participate in making decisions and
provides corresponding discLosure categories, the code fails to
iet out the miscellaneous provisions required by section 87302(b) .
This can be rectified by either extracting alI of that information
from the Act or by incorporating Commission Regulation 18730 as
the standard provisions of the code. Regulation 18730 sets out
aII of the niscellaneous provisions required by Section a7302,
including the disqualification provisions of section 87100. I
have enclosed a fact sheet which describes Regulation 18710 as
well as the procedure to incorporate its provisions into your
code.
The city did correct the disclosure categories of its code to
require the discLosure of business positions by its designated
poiitions. 
The category lor the Police Chief requires the
i j.sclosure of investnents and busj-ness positicns in business
entities, and income fron sources which proviCe services,
supplies, rnaterials, machinery or equipmenc oi the ti-pe utilized
by- bolice chief's depar!:neni. Ii Ehe bank r.s not of the type to
piovi.ae services of tne type u-.iIized by the cepart:len:, then it
is not necessary for the polrce chief tc disclcse h:s pcsition
wi.th the bank.
tle do have a concern whrch needs further rev j.ew cn your parc'
Is the disclosure assigned to the positions lj.sted as "Director or
Personnel" through "Fleet operations Foreman" suf f ici'ent? 
l.Je
noted that none of the positions are required to disclose
interests in real propeity. 
Can the Public Works SuPerintendent,
Wastewater Reclamation Superintendent or Transportation Officer
make or particiPate in decisions affectinq real property? If so,
then you must rlquire such disclosure f rom these positions as well
as any other positions ',rhich make decisions affecting real
property.
In your letter you stated that you were assr.iraing that the
revised Lode would noE af fec'- any previousty f iJ.ed statements. but
would onJ.y affect statements filed following the July 15, f991
amendment. That i-s correct. 
Designated employees do not need to
correct prevj,ousJ.y filed statements. The amended code became
effective on August 14, 1991, '{hich ',ras 30 days after adoption.
only positions newly designated in the ccde must file an initial
staternent 30 days f rom the ef :ecti../e date of the code. A11
positions existing in the code 'Jhen iE '.ras amended wi,11. continue
to file their annual statenents.


september 
■7′  ■99■
Page Four
I trust this has answered your questions sufficiently. 
If
there is anything further you need, please feel free to call me at
( 9I5) 322-5901.
Sincere IY,
Scott Ha l tabr in
Acting General Counse I
By: Jeanette E- Turvi 1I
PoIiticaI Reform consultant
Legal Division
sH/JEr/ j t
Enc I osure
